The coffee and food are REALLY good, and at really fair prices even in 2026. The staff is friendly and super accommodating. I love this place and will be a frequent flyer for sure!

We met for breakfast on a rainy Sunday morning. Wasn’t crowded which was nice.  Prices are reasonable. Food was good. Not great.  Nice quiet place to hang out with friends.  Interesting selection of books to read.  Friends just moved into a condo close by. I’m sure we’ll be back.  Proper Coffee cups for dine in would be a better touch vs paper cups.

reviewSample.text: This spot is gorgeous and so cozy! Customer service is TOP tier! What a lovely cafe! Tried the teddy bear latte and crème brûlée latte along with the breakfast burrito. The drinks are so CUTE and also yummy. Food was warm and delicious! I could not recommend more!

Traveled from Minnesota and Stopped by Toast Noir while we were in the area, and it ended up being a great little find. I ordered coffee for myself and got my girlfriend the strawberry cream matcha, and she absolutely loved it — super smooth, not too sweet, and really well made. My coffee was excellent too, rich flavor and perfectly balanced.

The place has a comfortable vibe with patio seating, which is great if you want to enjoy your drink outside. The location is convenient, easy to get to, and the overall atmosphere is clean, modern, and welcoming.

The coffee, food and service was amazing! I live about 8 minutes away, I wanted to find something close and cute and I  definitely hit the jack pot. I will definitely be coming back. Elhan was amazing. Best barista ever.

drink.name: Vanilla Lavender Latte
drink.reason: Specifically praised in a detailed review as the highlight, with subtle lavender, smooth espresso, and well-balanced flavor. Lattes generally are repeatedly praised across reviews.
drink.quote: latte was a definite highlight... lavender flavor was subtle and pleasant

food.reason: Only one food item (cinnamon donut) was mentioned, and it was described negatively as cold and dry. Not enough positive food data to recommend anything.

reviewSample.text: Cute Cafe with unique drinks and chill vibes. Cute and quiet atmosphere. Perfect for meeting with a friend or getting some work done. It was cold outside when I ate in the cafe and I kept my coat on the whole time I was there. Would have been nice if it was warmer but otherwise great little spot!

Perc Coffee Shop made a fantastic first impression! From the moment I walked in, I felt genuinely welcome. It’s rare and refreshing to have a real conversation with the barista while they’re making your coffee. Our chat wasn’t just about coffee—it was about life experiences, which added such a personal touch to the visit.

The latte I had was absolutely perfect, with just the right balance of coffee and milk, and the flavor was spot on. The space itself is cozy, especially in the winter when the outdoor seating is closed, but for those who can brave the cold, there are chairs outside as well. Inside, it’s warm, inviting, and vibrant.

What stood out most? The amazing service, the positive energy, and the welcoming vibe of the people working there. While I didn’t try any of their food this time, the coffee alone is worth the visit. Plus, it’s a beautiful, colorful spot to work or take a break from home. Highly recommend!

Cheers,

BN

"I recently visited Perc Coffee and tried their vanilla and lavender latte (Hot, without sugar) and a cinnamon donut.  The latte was a definite highlight. The lavender flavor was subtle and pleasant, not at all overpowering, and it paired beautifully with the vanilla. The coffee itself was well-made, not too sweet, and the espresso was smooth.  The price, I think was around $6, was reasonable for a specialty latte.
Unfortunately, the cinnamon donut was a bit of a letdown. While the flavor was good, it was served cold, which significantly detracted from the experience (they couldn't warm it up). The texture was also dry, making it even less enjoyable. A warm, fresh donut with a moist texture would have been much better. I also can't recall the price, but it's worth noting that the coldness and dryness impacted my overall impression.

I particularly liked the minimalist decor. The location is great, next to club pilates, fresh market, etc.
Despite the cold and dry donut, I would still recommend Perc primarily for their excellent lavender and vanilla latte. It's a great spot for a quiet coffee break, catching up with friends, or getting some work done.

The latte was the perfect temp, great tasting coffee, the syrup flavors were spot on. We took ours to go but I would love to come back to just sit for a bit! Highly highly highly recommend this coffee shop! Probably one of the best in Atlanta. Atmosphere is beautiful, spacious, and quiet. Coffee was extremely good. Barista was very kind! This will definitely be our coffee go-to when we’re in the area!!

reviewSample.text:
A quiet and clean sanctuary perfect for remote work, offering a calm atmosphere and plenty of dedicated workspaces. While the cold brew is standard, the overall quality and breakfast variety make it an instant favorite for anyone visiting Atlanta.
🌸
🌸
This is the perfect place to get some work done. It’s quiet, calm, and very clean, with well-designed working spaces that make it easy to focus. The coffee is delicious; while the cold brew didn’t necessarily blow me away, it is definitely a step up from any major coffee chain.
They also have a good selection of bread and breakfast options that look great—I’d love to come back and try everything on the menu. This was my first time in Atlanta, and finding a spot like this already has me in love with the city's coffee scene. Whether you need to be productive or just want a peaceful break, I highly recommend this spot.

This place is 10x better than main coffee chains. I think I found my new favorite coffee spot... calm, quiet atmosphere... friendly barista named Anna was everything  bring the kids to get their Hot Chocolate for the holidays it is to die for ... will definitely be a regular here."

The server saw me set up in the office and brought my tea to me.  Today Christmas music is playing in the background and it's warm and comfy in here. I will definitely come here more often

Great spot to stop after hiking by the Chattahoochee River. Located in a fairly new strip mall with Lots of parking available outside.

drink.name: Bananamelo Latte

reviewSample.text:
This was one of the most delicious flavored lattes I’ve had in a long time!! I tried the bananamelo, and I HIGHLY RECOMMEND. It was soooo good. Absolutely delicious. Sweet but not too sweet, and a good amount of espresso flavor in the latte portion. The topping was so delicious I could have eaten a bowl of it. My friend ordered the coquito iced coffee and that was very good also. I’d love to come back and try the fresas con crema too!!

Keep in mind, this is an experience, not something you can just pop in for and grab something quickly! We came at around 11ish on a Saturday and we had to wait in line for around 30 minutes to order, but our drinks came out so quickly. It was worth it though! And there is some nice outdoor seating where we were able to chill for a while and study. Also, there isn’t a sign for Enid Coffee outside the building — just go inside the Comunidad Taqueria and jump in the line on the right side of the entrance.

Tbh BEST matcha I’ve had in the city!! I’m sooo picky about my matcha and usually only go to Doraville/Duluth for it but glad I found a good place to go in the city!! I got the banana matcha and wowwww the banana flavor is strong and not that artificial banana taste either. Also the line was pretty long but it moved fast. Service was great too . Will be back!!! :)))

Best coffee and matcha I’ve ever had. Even LA and NYC haven’t topped it. Atlanta is lucky to have this place.
Pro tip: go ahead and order 2 cause you’re gonna want another one lol

Had a delicious breakfast sandwich and latte. The seating area is clean and comfortable and I enjoyed great music that paired with great food. Staff are very friendly and helpful. I will definitely be back.

reviewSample.text: Inside a Ritz-Carlton. Tiny coffee stand with about 5 small tables. Was after drip coffee and prefer a medium-dark roast. Barista had on hand light and dark roast drip options. I got the dark. It's decent. Beats Starbucks, which is mostly what I was after. I really came because I needed breakfast too and luckily they have breakfast sandwiches. I was pleasantly surprised that the biscuit was soft and crumbly. I got the turkey sausage, cheese and egg, which tasted exactly as you can imagine but I guess way better than generic commercial cafe or fast food. Mostly this place delivered what I needed. Not the most amazing coffee, but given the options around it is probably the best option. I will say, the barista on hand was super friendly and delightful. When I thought the spicy burrito was the only option, she helpfully informed me that she doesn't really recommend the pre-made burritos and instead directed me to the freshly made breakfast sandwiches, which indeed turned out to be perfect for me.

Excellent, kind service with a great recommendation. I got their drip coffee, banana bread (banana and maple spice) cold brew, and yogurt parfait. Everything was so good. The granola for the parfait was amazing and the yogurt had a nice tang. The coffees were smooth and made not too sweet. Highly recommend you check this place out

Coming from NYC, I was very impressed by both the coffee and the outstanding service. It was a Monday morning, I was sleep-deprived, and the baristas were incredibly pleasant and helpful. The espresso was excellent, and the chicken chorizo breakfast burrito was good—there’s only so much you can do given the small space, but the quality was solid. The pricing for the food felt appropriate, and the quality of the espresso for the price exceeded my expectations. The service really stood out, and I’d strongly recommend this spot to any traveler looking for a reliable coffee stop after either a rough night or a good one.

If you need a quiet spot to work or study away from home, this is the place.

This is one of the best coffee shops I’ve ever been to. I needed a place to work for a couple hours before my flight, and the coffee, food, and atmosphere were incredible. The barista was so incredibly kind, and there was plenty of comfortable space to spread out and work. They had no issue with me staying a while.
Everything was pretty cheap too, despite how genuinely delicious it was. This isn’t second-rate coffee, you won’t be disappointed. They’ve got signs pointing to exactly where you need to walk, so while it is a bit of a “hidden gem”, don’t worry about getting lost.

reviewSample.text: No frills coffee shop hits the spot in the morning.  the outdoor seating is probably more comfortable as the sun comes in the windows.  Knowledgeable baristas keep it real without any flavored drinks.  Be prepared for a little wait if you have food, but that's to be expected.

Was very much looking forward to having my first coffee here at Blue donkey. Walked in with my wife it's open there was nobody in line and even though this is a coffee shop I could not order a coffee because one half hour before they are due to close they clean the espresso machine. I've never heard of this in my life. This kind of maintenance should be done after you close not before you close. I own several nightclubs and this is just an odd situation. You're obviously missing money since my wife and I walked out and probably won't be back.

Hope your business plan succeeds with your weird cleaning policy but I doubt it will.

A good cappuccino is more than the wonderful taste and brilliant aroma. A good cappuccino allows a person to ground itself.  To find a bit of respite in an otherwise hectic day.  This is the simplicity and importance of the serving mug.  The vehicle by which you enjoy that bit of deliciousness conveys to the drinker "take a minute to enjoy the moment, and collect yourself."

At Blue Donkey, the purchaser is offered a choice between a 12 ounce or a 16 ounce cappuccino. Strike one for me.  Then, as many coffee shops have done, Blu Donkey eliminated the ceramic conveyor in favor of the take away paper. Rush, rush, rush with your cappuccino. Strike two.

However, I must be honest in sharing that the flavor was very good.  And, while the presentation was lacking, my barista acknowledged the delivery could have been better.  Fair point. All in, I'd certainly stop by Blue Donkey again. Perhaps with my own serving wear.

reviewSample.text:
The space is really cute and a nice spot to sit and get some work done. That said, it doesn’t quite feel like a true coffee shop. The drinks are made using automated machines where they just press “latte” or “americano,” and unfortunately my americano tasted pretty bland.

It’s located inside a residential building, which might convenient for people who live there and just want a quick grab-and-go coffee. But I wouldn’t make a special trip here if you’re looking for a quality coffee experience.

The barista (I think her name was Crista) was exceptionally kind, helpful, and made a mean drink to boot. Also, their pastries are super tasty!

Quiet area with lots of space, but enough activity that it isn’t too quiet. Would recommend to anyone in the area who can walk to it (parking isn’t free)!

I generally don’t write reviews, but everything about this coffee shop is excellent! The danish was delicious and Kevin can make a mean pour-over that hits all the right notes. He is extremely personable and made the overall experience much more enjoyable. Highlight of my trip.
